title: AI Problem Framing
definition: Deciding whether AI/ML is the right approach vs rules, search, or manual processes.
frameworkSteps: Assess if AI adds unique value
interviewPrompts: When would you NOT use AI for this problem?
officeUse: AI feature proposals and build vs buy decisions.

title: AI Eval Criteria
definition: Measurable quality bars for AI outputs: accuracy, relevance, safety, latency, cost.
whenToUse: Before shipping any AI feature; ongoing regression testing.
interviewPrompts: How would you evaluate an AI summarization feature?
officeUse: AI PRDs, launch checklists, and monitoring dashboards.

title: Human-in-the-Loop
definition: Designing human review, correction, or override into AI workflows.
whenToUse: High-stakes outputs, early launches, or low model confidence.
interviewPrompts: Where would you add human review in this AI flow?
officeUse: AI product design and trust/safety planning.

title: Golden Datasets
definition: Curated input-output pairs used to benchmark AI quality over time.
whenToUse: Building and maintaining AI eval pipelines.
interviewPrompts: How do you build a golden set for X?

title: AI Regression Testing
definition: Re-running evals on golden datasets after model or prompt changes to catch quality drops.
whenToUse: Every model update, prompt change, or context window change.
frameworkSteps: Run eval suite on golden set
Block release if critical metrics drop
interviewPrompts: How do you prevent AI quality regressions?
